law.text:
Chapter 4. Succession entitlement of the spouse and the legally cohabiting partner

Section 1. The surviving spouse

Art. 4.17. Inheritance rights of the surviving spouse

par. 1. When the deceased leaves descendants, adopted children or descendants of these, the surviving spouse acquires the usufruct of the entire estate.

par. 2. When the deceased leaves relatives in the ascending line or close collateral relatives, the surviving spouse acquires:
(1) the full ownership of the share of the first to die in the common property and in the property held exclusively in undivided co-ownership between the spouses, and
(2) the usufruct of the remaining goods of their own property.
(3) the usufruct of the goods subject to the right of legal reversion referred to in article 4.24 or in article 4.25, unless otherwise provided in the deed of donation or in the will.

par. 3. When the deceased leaves other heirs or no heirs, the surviving spouse acquires the full ownership of the entire estate.

Art. 4.18. Usufruct over donated goods

Upon the death of the donor, the surviving spouse who is called to the estate receives the usufruct of the goods that the donor donated and of which the donor reserved the usufruct, on the condition that the spouse already held that capacity at the time of the donation and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until the day of their death.

Upon the death of the donor, the surviving spouse who is called to the estate receives the usufruct of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate opened and of the household effects present therein, on the condition that the donor had reserved the usufruct of those goods upon the donation thereof, that the spouse was legally cohabiting with the donor at the time of the donation, and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until the day of their death.

The spouse may renounce that usufruct. Articles 4.244 to 4.253 apply to such renunciation when it takes place during the lifetime of the donor.

Art. 4.20. Right to lease

The surviving spouse acquires exclusively, to the exclusion of all other heirs, the right to the lease of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the deceased opened.

Art. 4.22. Exclusion or forfeiture

par. 1. The surviving spouse may be wholly or partially excluded from or declared to have forfeited their inheritance rights if they have been wholly or partially deprived of parental authority over the children born of their marriage with the deceased.

par. 2. The legal action is brought by the descendants within one year calculated either from the opening of the estate or from the deprivation of parental authority.

The judgment takes effect from the date on which the action was brought.

par. 3. If the usufruct has already been converted into the full ownership of a good or into a sum of money, or if the bare ownership of the goods referred to in article 4.61, paragraph 7, has already been transferred, the exclusion or declaration of forfeiture gives rise to a right to compensation.

That compensation is determined by the court and corresponds to the value of the usufruct, having regard also to the presumed life expectancy of the usufructuary at the time the legal action was brought.

If the usufruct has been converted into a life annuity, the judgment has retroactive effect to the same point in time.

Section 2. The surviving legally cohabiting partner

Art. 4.23. Inheritance rights and right to lease of the surviving legally cohabiting partner

par. 1. The surviving legally cohabiting partner acquires, regardless of which heirs they are called to the estate with, the usufruct of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the deceased opened, and of the household effects present therein.

The surviving legally cohabiting partner also exercises this right if the goods referred to in the first paragraph are the subject of a right of legal reversion referred to in article 4.24 or in article 4.25.

par. 2. The surviving legally cohabiting partner who is called to the estate receives upon the death of the donor the usufruct of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate opened and of the household effects present therein, if the donor had reserved the usufruct of these goods upon the donation thereof, and on the condition that the legally cohabiting partner already held that capacity at the time of the donation and that the donor remained the holder of this usufruct until the day of their death.

The legally cohabiting partner may renounce the usufruct referred to in the first paragraph. Articles 4.244 to 4.253 apply to such renunciation when it takes place during the lifetime of the donor.

par. 3. The surviving legally cohabiting partner acquires exclusively, to the exclusion of all other heirs, the right to the lease of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the predeceased legally cohabiting partner opened, and acquires the usufruct of the household effects present therein.

par. 4. The foregoing provisions do not apply when the surviving legally cohabiting partner is a descendant of the predeceased legally cohabiting partner.

par. 5. Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary, any person who acquires the bare ownership may require that an inventory of the household effects and a statement of condition of the immovable property that served as the family's principal residence be drawn up.

par. 6. The rules concerning the usufruct of the surviving spouse contained in articles 4.22 and 4.60 to 4.64 apply mutatis mutandis to the usufruct of the surviving legally cohabiting partner.

kb_used.fo:
vocabulary V {
  type Person
  type Property
  is_surviving_spouse_of: Person * Person -> Bool
  is_heir_of: Person * Person -> Bool
  served_as_family_principal_residence: Property * Person -> Bool
  property_is_leased: Property -> Bool
  acquires_right_to_lease: Person * Property -> Bool
  excluded_from_lease_right: Person * Property -> Bool
}

theory T:V {
  ! spouse in Person, deceased in Person, prop in Property: (((is_surviving_spouse_of(spouse,deceased)) & (served_as_family_principal_residence(prop,deceased)) & (property_is_leased(prop)))) => ((acquires_right_to_lease(spouse,prop))).
  ! heir in Person, spouse in Person, deceased in Person, prop in Property: (((is_heir_of(heir,deceased)) & (is_surviving_spouse_of(spouse,deceased)) & (acquires_right_to_lease(spouse,prop)))) => ((excluded_from_lease_right(heir,prop))).
}

case.text: Willem and Yara were married. Yara dies. At the time the estate opened, the apartment in Ghent was the principal family home.

questions.text: Does Willem acquire, pursuant to Article 4.20, the right to the tenancy of the family home as the sole beneficiary?

expected.reason: Art. 4.20: the surviving spouse exclusively obtains the rental right of the real estate that served as the family home.

is_surviving_spouse_of.name: is_surviving_spouse_of
is_surviving_spouse_of.description: The first person is the surviving spouse of the second person (the deceased). This is a background status that may be directly asserted in a case.

is_heir_of.name: is_heir_of
is_heir_of.description: The first person is an heir of the second person (the deceased).

served_as_family_principal_residence.name: served_as_family_principal_residence
served_as_family_principal_residence.description: The property served as the family's principal residence at the time the estate of the deceased opened.

property_is_leased.name: property_is_leased
property_is_leased.description: The property is subject to a lease agreement.

acquires_right_to_lease.name: acquires_right_to_lease
acquires_right_to_lease.description: The surviving spouse acquires exclusively the right to the lease of the property, to the exclusion of all other heirs.

excluded_from_lease_right.name: excluded_from_lease_right
excluded_from_lease_right.description: The heir is excluded from the right to the lease of the property because the surviving spouse acquires it exclusively.

routing.question: Does Willem acquire, pursuant to Article 4.20, the right to the tenancy of the family home as the sole beneficiary?

deduction.label: unknown
deduction.constraint: ? x0 in Person, x1 in Property: __sel0(x0) & __sel1(x1) & acquires_right_to_lease(x0,x1)
